title: Society for Human Rights founded in Chicago
description: Henry Gerber established the Society for Human Rights in Chicago, recognized as the earliest documented gay rights organization in the United States.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Mattachine Society formed as an early gay rights group
description: Activist Harry Hay helped launch the Mattachine Society, among the first sustained gay rights groups in the country, focused on social acceptance and support for homosexual people.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: US psychiatry classifies homosexuality as a disorder
description: The American Psychiatric Association’s diagnostic manual categorized homosexuality as a sociopathic personality disturbance.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Eisenhower bars gay people from federal employment
description: President Dwight D. Eisenhower issued an executive order barring homosexual people from federal government jobs, casting them as security risks.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Daughters of Bilitis founded in San Francisco
description: The Daughters of Bilitis formed in San Francisco as the first known lesbian rights organization in the country, holding private gatherings out of fear of police raids and discrimination.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Illinois first US state to repeal its sodomy law
description: Illinois repealed its sodomy statute, becoming the first US state to decriminalize consensual same-sex conduct.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: First US televised documentary on homosexuality airs
description: A California local station broadcast what is regarded as the first US-televised documentary about homosexuality.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Police raid on the Stonewall Inn sparks days of protest
description: A police raid on the Stonewall Inn in New York City set off protests and demonstrations widely seen as the catalyst for the modern gay civil rights movement in the country.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Los Angeles Advocate renamed The Advocate
description: The publication begun in 1967 as the Los Angeles Advocate took the name The Advocate; it is considered the oldest continuing LGBTQ periodical in the country.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: First gay pride march held in New York City
description: On the first anniversary of the Stonewall uprising, community members marched through New York City streets in an event called Christopher Street Liberation Day, now regarded as the first gay pride parade.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Lambda Legal established as first LGBTQ legal group
description: Lambda Legal became the first legal organization created to advance the rights of gay and lesbian people; after being denied nonprofit status, it won recognition through a New York court ruling.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Maryland first US state to ban same-sex marriage
description: Maryland enacted the first US state statute explicitly prohibiting marriage between same-sex couples.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: First meeting of the group that became PFLAG
description: Parents and Friends of Gays held its first meeting, the start of an organization that expanded nationally as Parents, Families and Friends of Lesbians and Gays in 1982.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: American Psychiatric Association declassifies homosexuality
description: By a membership vote, the American Psychiatric Association removed homosexuality from its list of mental disorders in the DSM-II, reversing decades of pathologization.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Kathy Kozachenko wins seat on Ann Arbor council
description: Kathy Kozachenko won a seat on the Ann Arbor, Michigan city council, becoming the first openly LGBTQ American elected to public office.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Elaine Noble elected to Massachusetts legislature
description: Elaine Noble became the first openly gay candidate elected to a US state office when voters sent her to the Massachusetts legislature.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: First federal gay rights bill introduced in Congress
description: The first federal bill addressing discrimination based on sexual orientation was introduced; it went to committee but was never brought forward for consideration.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Leonard Matlovich discharged after coming out
description: Air Force Technical Sergeant and decorated Vietnam veteran Leonard Matlovich disclosed his sexual orientation to his commanding officer and was discharged months later; a 1980 appeals ruling found the dismissal improper and restored his back pay.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Renee Richards challenges ban from women’s US Open
description: Tennis player Renee Richards was barred from the women’s US Open under a women-born-women rule after her 1975 surgery; a New York court ruled in her favor in 1977, and she competed that year.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Harvey Milk takes office as San Francisco supervisor
description: Harvey Milk was inaugurated as a San Francisco city supervisor, the first openly gay man elected to office in California; that November he and Mayor George Moscone were assassinated by former supervisor Dan White.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Gilbert Baker creates the first rainbow flag
description: Artist Gilbert Baker designed and sewed the first rainbow flag as a symbol of pride and hope for the LGBTQ community.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: First National March on Washington for gay rights
description: The first National March on Washington for Lesbian and Gay Rights drew an estimated 75,000 to 125,000 people demanding equal rights.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Wisconsin bans anti-gay discrimination, a US first
description: Wisconsin became the first US state to outlaw discrimination based on sexual orientation.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Lambda Legal wins first HIV/AIDS discrimination case
description: In People v. West 12 Tenants Corp., Lambda Legal won what is regarded as the first HIV/AIDS discrimination lawsuit, after neighbors sought to evict a doctor treating HIV-positive patients.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: First National Coming Out Day observed
description: The first National Coming Out Day was marked in the country, promoting visibility for LGBTQ people.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Clinton signs Don’t Ask, Don’t Tell military policy
description: President Bill Clinton signed the Don’t Ask, Don’t Tell directive, barring openly gay and lesbian people from military service while restricting harassment of closeted personnel; the policy was repealed on September 20, 2011.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Hate crime sentencing law adds sexual orientation
description: The Hate Crimes Sentencing Enhancement Act took effect, letting judges impose harsher sentences when a victim was targeted because of characteristics including sexual orientation.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Clinton signs the Defense of Marriage Act
description: President Bill Clinton signed the Defense of Marriage Act, denying federal recognition to same-sex marriage and defining marriage as a union of one man and one woman.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Hawaii court backs marriage rights for gay couples
description: A Hawaii judge ruled the state could not deny same-sex couples the right to marry, making Hawaii the first state to recognize that gay and lesbian couples were entitled to the same privileges as heterosexual married couples.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Ellen DeGeneres comes out on Time magazine cover
description: Comedian Ellen DeGeneres publicly came out as a lesbian on the cover of Time magazine.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Ellen character is first TV lead to come out
description: On the series Ellen, the lead character Ellen Morgan came out, the first leading character to do so on a prime-time US network television show.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Coretta Scott King calls for ending homophobia
description: Coretta Scott King urged the civil rights community to join the effort to end homophobia.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Matthew Shepard fatally beaten near Laramie
description: Matthew Shepard was tied to a fence and severely beaten near Laramie, Wyoming, and died of his injuries days later; the killing drew national attention to anti-gay violence.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Shepard’s attackers make first court appearance
description: Russell Henderson and Aaron McKinney made their first court appearance after their arrest in the attack on Matthew Shepard; each later received two life sentences.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Lawrence v. Texas strikes down US sodomy laws
description: In Lawrence v. Texas, the US Supreme Court struck down remaining sodomy laws, decriminalizing consensual same-sex conduct nationwide and overturning the 1986 Bowers v. Hardwick decision.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: First legal same-sex marriage in the United States
description: Massachusetts became the site of the first legal same-sex marriage in the United States.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: California legislature passes marriage bill, then vetoed
description: California’s legislature became the first to pass a bill allowing marriage between same-sex couples, but Governor Arnold Schwarzenegger vetoed it.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: New Jersey court orders marriage rights for gay couples
description: The New Jersey Supreme Court ruled that state lawmakers had to extend the rights and benefits of marriage to gay and lesbian couples.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: California high court backs same-sex marriage
description: The California Supreme Court ruled that limiting marriage to opposite-sex couples was unconstitutional.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: California voters pass Proposition 8
description: California voters approved Proposition 8, banning same-sex marriage; a federal judge later found the measure unconstitutional.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Harvey Milk honored with Medal of Freedom
description: President Barack Obama posthumously awarded Harvey Milk the Presidential Medal of Freedom.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Obama signs Matthew Shepard hate crimes act
description: President Barack Obama signed the Matthew Shepard and James Byrd Jr. Hate Crimes Prevention Act, extending federal hate-crime protections to sexual orientation and gender identity.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Obama becomes first president to back marriage equality
description: In a television interview, Barack Obama became the first sitting US president to publicly support the freedom of same-sex couples to marry.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Democratic Party backs marriage equality in platform
description: The Democratic Party became the first major US political party to endorse same-sex marriage in its national platform at its convention.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Tammy Baldwin elected to the US Senate
description: Tammy Baldwin became the first openly gay person and the first woman from Wisconsin elected to the US Senate.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: US v. Windsor strikes down part of DOMA
description: In United States v. Windsor, the US Supreme Court struck down a core section of the Defense of Marriage Act, granting federal benefits to legally married same-sex couples.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Supreme Court lets marriage rulings stand in five states
description: The US Supreme Court declined to review five marriage cases, letting lower-court rulings stand and enabling same-sex couples to marry in Utah, Oklahoma, Virginia, Indiana and Wisconsin, with more states to follow.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Obergefell v. Hodges brings marriage equality nationwide
description: After hearing oral arguments in April, the US Supreme Court ruled on June 26 that states could not ban same-sex marriage, establishing marriage equality across the country in a 5-4 decision.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: US military opportunity policy covers gay members
description: Defense Secretary Ash Carter announced that the Military Equal Opportunity policy had been expanded to cover gay and lesbian service members.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Boy Scouts lifts ban on openly gay leaders
description: Boy Scouts of America announced that its national board had removed the restriction barring openly gay adults from serving as leaders and employees.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Eric Fanning confirmed as first gay Army secretary
description: The Senate confirmed Eric Fanning as secretary of the Army, making him the first openly gay leader of a US military branch.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Stonewall named first national LGBTQ monument
description: President Barack Obama designated the Stonewall National Monument, the first US national monument dedicated to LGBTQ rights, covering the sites of the 1969 uprising.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Pentagon lifts ban on open transgender service
description: Defense Secretary Ash Carter announced the Pentagon would lift its ban on transgender people serving openly in the US military.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Record number of out athletes at Rio Olympics
description: The Rio de Janeiro Summer Olympics featured a record number of openly LGB athletes, estimated at more than 40, up from about 23 at the 2012 London Games.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Kate Brown becomes governor of Oregon
description: Kate Brown was sworn in as governor of Oregon, becoming the highest-ranking openly LGBTQ person elected to office in the country.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Court says Civil Rights Act covers LGBTQ workers
description: The 7th Circuit Court of Appeals ruled that the Civil Rights Act bars workplace discrimination against LGBTQ employees, in a case brought by Kimberly Hively against Ivy Tech Community College.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Washington DC offers gender-neutral ID marker
description: Residents of the District of Columbia became the first in the country able to select X as a gender marker instead of male or female on driver’s licenses and identification cards.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Danica Roem elected to Virginia legislature
description: Danica Roem won a seat in the Virginia House of Delegates, becoming the first openly transgender candidate elected to a US state legislature.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: First openly transgender Academy Awards presenter
description: Actor Daniela Vega became the first openly transgender presenter in Academy Awards history when she introduced a performance during the ceremony.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Jared Polis elected first openly gay governor
description: Jared Polis won the Colorado governor’s race, becoming the first openly gay man elected governor in the country.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: New York bans the gay and trans panic defense
description: New York Governor Andrew Cuomo signed a law barring the gay and trans panic defense, making New York the sixth state to prohibit the tactic.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Billy Porter wins lead drama actor Emmy
description: Billy Porter became the first openly gay Black man to win the Emmy for best lead actor in a drama series.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Court orders Idaho to provide an inmate’s surgery
description: The 9th Circuit Court of Appeals upheld a ruling that Idaho must provide gender confirmation surgery to incarcerated person Adree Edmo, the first time a federal appeals court required a state to provide such care to an inmate.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Bostock ruling protects LGBTQ workers nationwide
description: The US Supreme Court ruled that federal law protects LGBTQ workers from job discrimination, extending Title VII protections to millions of workers across the country.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Court backs transgender student Gavin Grimm
description: The 4th Circuit Court of Appeals ruled in favor of former student Gavin Grimm, finding that segregating transgender students through restroom policies is unconstitutional and violates federal law.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Election delivers several LGBTQ firsts to office
description: The general election produced multiple firsts: Sarah McBride became the first openly transgender state senator in Delaware, Ritchie Torres and Mondaire Jones the first out gay Black men elected to Congress in New York, and Mauree Turner the first nonbinary state legislator in Oklahoma.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Pete Buttigieg confirmed as first gay Cabinet member
description: The Senate confirmed Pete Buttigieg as secretary of transportation, making him the first openly gay Cabinet member confirmed by the chamber.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Rachel Levine confirmed as first trans federal official
description: The Senate confirmed Dr. Rachel Levine as an assistant secretary for health, the first openly transgender federal official confirmed by the chamber.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Carl Nassib is first active NFL player to come out
description: Las Vegas Raiders defensive lineman Carl Nassib became the first active NFL player to publicly announce he is gay.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: State Department allows self-selected passport sex marker
description: The State Department said it would let passport applicants self-select their sex marker and would drop medical certification requirements when the selection did not match other identity documents.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Social Security lets people choose their sex marker
description: The Social Security Administration announced that people could choose their sex marker in their Social Security records.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Biden signs the Respect for Marriage Act
description: President Joe Biden signed the Respect for Marriage Act, adding federal protections for same-sex and interracial couples, voiding the Defense of Marriage Act, and requiring states to honor out-of-state marriage licenses.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Tennessee bans gender-affirming care for minors
description: Tennessee Governor Bill Lee signed a law prohibiting gender-affirming care for minors; the ban later took effect and, in June 2025, the US Supreme Court upheld it.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: FDA eases blood donation rules for gay and bi men
description: The FDA finalized risk-based recommendations that removed deferrals and screening questions specific to men who have sex with men, allowing more gay and bisexual men to donate blood.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: First out gay coach in US men’s pro sports
description: Jacksonville Jaguars strength coach Kevin Maxen became the first male coach in a US men’s professional sports league to come out as gay.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Laphonza Butler joins the US Senate
description: Laphonza Butler, appointed to represent California, became the first known Black lesbian in the US Senate and the first out LGBTQ member of the Judiciary Committee.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Biden rule expands LGBTQ health care protections
description: The Biden administration announced a rule broadening safeguards against discrimination for gay and transgender people seeking medical care, reversing earlier limits on federal health protections.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Sarah McBride elected first trans member of Congress
description: Delaware state senator Sarah McBride won a US House seat, becoming the first openly transgender person elected to Congress.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Trump order restricts gender markers on passports
description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order rolling back gender identity designations, and the State Department suspended issuing passports with an X marker; in November 2025 the Supreme Court allowed passport sex markers to be required to match biological sex.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Trump orders ban on transgender service members
description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order barring transgender people from military service; after courts intervened, the Supreme Court in May 2025 allowed the administration to enforce the ban.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Trump order targets gender-affirming care for youth
description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order ending federal support for institutions that provide gender-affirming care to transgender youth; a federal judge blocked the order.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Trump order restricts trans women in sports
description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order titled "Keeping Men Out of Women’s Sports" aimed at barring transgender women from women’s athletic competition.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts
